source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to IAM policies will help ensure authentication and authorization controls remain intact. Impact: Monitoring these changes may result in a number of "false positives," especially in larger environments. This alert may require more tuning than others to eliminate some of those erroneous notifications.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{($.eventName=DeleteGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteRolePolicy)||($.e ventName=DeleteUserPolicy)||($.eventName=PutGroupPolicy)||($.eventName= PutRolePolicy)||($.eventName=PutUserPolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicy) ||($.eventName=DeletePolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicyVersion)||($.eve ntName=DeletePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=AttachRolePolicy)||($.eventNa me=DetachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=AttachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=Deta chUserPolicy)||($.eventName=AttachGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachGrou pPolicy)}" 4. Note the <iam-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <iam-change-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<iam- changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for IAM policy changes and the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name `<trail-log-group-name>` -- filter-name `<iam-changes-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<iam-changes-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 -- filter-pattern '{($.eventName=DeleteGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteRolePolicy)||($.e ventName=DeleteUserPolicy)||($.eventName=PutGroupPolicy)||($.eventName= PutRolePolicy)||($.eventName=PutUserPolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicy) ||($.eventName=DeletePolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicyVersion)||($.eve ntName=DeletePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=AttachRolePolicy)||($.eventNa me=DetachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=AttachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=Deta chUserPolicy)||($.eventName=AttachGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachGrou pPolicy)}' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<iam-changes-alarm>` -- metric-name `<iam-changes-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 -- threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold -- evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns- topic-arn> References: 1. CCE-79189-7 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.impact: Monitoring these changes may result in a number of "false positives," especially in larger environments. This alert may require more tuning than others to eliminate some of those erroneous notifications.
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79189-7 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control involves configuring and verifying AWS services such as CloudTrail and CloudWatch, which can be fully checked using AWS APIs.
- AWS CLI commands and boto3 SDKs provide necessary methods to query the configuration of CloudTrail trails, CloudWatch metric filters, and alarms.
- The audit process is primarily based on verifying the existence and configuration of metric filters, alarms, and SNS topics using AWS CLI commands, suggesting that the entire process can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_iam_policy_changes_monitored

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all CloudTrail trails using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`.
- Identify trails with `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to `True`.
- Verify that such trails have logging enabled using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()` with `IsLogging=True`.
- Check event selectors for the trail using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()` to ensure they include management events with `IncludeManagementEvents=True` and `ReadWriteType=All`.
- List associated metric filters using `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters()` for the relevant log group.
- Ensure that the `filterPattern` includes specific IAM events as described in the benchmark.
- Verify alarms using `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` and cross-check with the metric name from the metric filter.
- Confirm SNS subscriptions are active using `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`, ensuring at least one valid subscription exists.

This approach ensures that all required configurations for monitoring IAM policy changes are correctly implemented and active across AWS services, aligning with the CIS benchmark's guidance for securing IAM policy changes via monitoring.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control involves verifying configurations that can be fully checked using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI. The tasks involve querying AWS services like CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS, which are well-supported by AWS SDK capabilities.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of boto3 methods such as `describe_trails()`, `get_trail_status()`, `get_event_selectors()`, `describe_metric_filters()`, `describe_alarms()`, and `list_subscriptions_by_topic()` are valid and appropriate for the tasks described.
- However, the program name `aws_cloudtrail_iam_policy_changes_monitored` could be more descriptive. It should ideally reflect the specific checks being performed, such as the inclusion of metric filters and alarms.
- The description of verifying the `filterPattern` for specific IAM events should be more explicit about the exact events being monitored, as per the CIS benchmark.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudtrail_iam_policy_change_monitoring` to better reflect the specific focus on IAM policy changes.
- **Missing Considerations:** Ensure that the `filterPattern` accurately captures all relevant IAM policy change events. The CIS benchmark typically specifies exact event names or patterns, which should be explicitly mentioned.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules to complement this approach, as AWS Config can provide a continuous assessment of compliance with configurations.
- **Documentation:** Include a step to document findings and any discrepancies found during the audit for compliance reporting.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded in AWS capabilities, and the automation approach is technically sound. The use of boto3 and AWS CLI is appropriate for the tasks described.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name to be more descriptive and ensure that the `filterPattern` explicitly matches the IAM policy change events as specified in the CIS benchmark.
- Consider adding AWS Config as a complementary tool for continuous compliance monitoring.
- Ensure documentation of the audit process and findings for compliance purposes.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required checks—verifying CloudTrail multi-region trails, CloudWatch metric filters, alarms, and SNS subscriptions—can be performed using AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The audit and remediation steps map directly to API calls, and all relevant configuration properties are exposed programmatically. No manual inspection or console-only steps are required.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_iam_policy_change_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS Topics
- **API/CLI Methods:**
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()`
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
- **Properties to Check:**
  - At least one CloudTrail trail with `IsMultiRegionTrail=True` and `IsLogging=True`
  - Trail includes management events with `IncludeManagementEvents=True` and `ReadWriteType=All`
  - CloudTrail is sending logs to a CloudWatch Log Group
  - CloudWatch Log Group has a metric filter with a filter pattern matching IAM policy change events (e.g., `$.eventName=CreatePolicy`, `$.eventName=Put*Policy`, `$.eventName=DeletePolicy`, etc.)
  - CloudWatch Alarm exists for the metric filter and is enabled
  - Alarm notifies an active SNS topic with at least one confirmed subscription
- **Pass Criteria:**
  - All above properties are present and correctly configured as per CIS benchmark requirements
- **Fail Criteria:**
  - Any required property is missing, misconfigured, or not enabled (e.g., no multi-region trail, missing metric filter, alarm not linked to metric, SNS topic not subscribed)

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation, with only minor differences in program naming and emphasis on filter pattern specificity. The final program name is made more descriptive per the second opinion’s suggestion, and automation details explicitly require the filter pattern to match all IAM policy change events as per CIS guidance.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
